# Register @neo-gpt-emmy in the canonical maintainer roster

## Context

The operator is creating `neo-gpt-emmy` as the second GPT-family maintainer account. The peer-naming round converged on **Emmy**, but the bearer cannot assent or veto until first boot; therefore the committed onboarding state must preserve the Layer-1 operational identity and leave the Social Name unresolved.

The repo already ships the dry-run-first four-surface generator from #14916. This ticket is the first concrete GPT-sibling application of that substrate.

## The Problem

Until `@neo-gpt-emmy` is registered, the new resident has no canonical AgentIdentity root, no public roster row, no pending capability record, and no executable roster pin. Booting first and repairing those surfaces afterward would invert the onboarding gate and risk identity/mailbox drift.

## Architectural Reality

- `ai/graph/identityRoots.mjs` is the committed AgentIdentity roster and Memory Core boot-time seed source.
- `ai/scripts/setup/generateRosterOnboarding.mjs` owns the coherent four-surface ceremony: identity root, README roster row, ModelStats pending section, and identityRoots spec pin.
- Engine facts are observation-owned. The onboarding input surface deliberately rejects model/designation/capability values; GPT-5.6 Sol facts land only after the live harness is observed.
- Social Names are bearer-assented. The onboarding input surface deliberately rejects `name` / `socialName`; Emmy is recorded only after first-boot assent. Until then, the root uses the handle-derived display form and README uses `-`.
- A second Codex resident needs an isolated `CODEX_HOME`, checkout, credentials, identity binding, mailbox, and runtime wake self-registration. It must not inherit Euclid's state or static route.

## The Fix

Run the canonical generator for `@neo-gpt-emmy` / family `gpt`, review its four-file payload, and land the pending-first-boot resident:

1. `ai/graph/identityRoots.mjs` — version-free `@neo-gpt-emmy` identity, `siblingOf: '@neo-gpt'`, separate mailbox/memory contract, no engine facts, no static subscription template, non-active onboarding status.
2. `README.md` — machine-account row with no Social Name or engine prediction.
3. `learn/agentos/ModelStats.md` — pending identity skeleton with source placeholders.
4. `test/playwright/unit/ai/graph/identityRoots.spec.mjs` — Layer-1 roster pin and absence checks for observation-owned facts.

Add the onboarding PR to ModelStats update history after its number exists.

## Contract Ledger

| Target | Source of Authority | Behavior | Failure posture | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| AgentIdentity root | `identityRoots.mjs` + generator contract | durable `@neo-gpt-emmy` resident, separate from `@neo-gpt` | refuse duplicate/colliding handle or missing anchor | focused roster spec |
| Social Name | [Discussion #11240](https://github.com/orgs/neomjs/discussions/11240) + generator rejection contract | handle-derived display until first-boot assent | never seed Emmy as an assented fact | generated diff + absence of social-name input |
| Engine facts | ModelStats observation discipline | remain pending until live first boot | no predicted designation/capabilities | spec absence assertions |
| Wake/instance isolation | Fleet onboarding contract | own CODEX_HOME, checkout, mailbox, runtime route | remain non-active until live proof | activation prerequisites |

## Decision Record Impact

Aligned with ADR-0032's durable-resident identity grain and the merged #14916 generator contract. No new architectural decision.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `@neo-gpt-emmy` exists exactly once in `IDENTITIES` with canonical GitHub login and mailbox.
- [ ] The identity contract records `siblingOf: '@neo-gpt'`, same-family review semantics, separate memory provenance, and isolated-harness prerequisites.
- [ ] Participation remains non-active until first-boot identity binding and wake self-registration are proven.
- [ ] No Social Name is committed before Emmy's bearer assent; no model/engine/capability facts are predicted.
- [ ] README, ModelStats pending section, and the roster spec are synchronized with the root.
- [ ] The focused generator and identityRoots specs pass.
- [ ] The PR targets `dev` and receives cross-family review.

## Out of Scope

GitHub account creation/WRITE grant (operator-owned); Codex login and credentials; first-boot execution; Social Name activation flip; observed engine/capability fill; merge execution.

## Avoided Traps

- **Copy Euclid's runtime route or data home** — rejected; siblings require isolated state and runtime self-registration.
- **Pre-fill GPT-5.6 Sol facts because the intended engine is known** — rejected; runtime observation owns the episode.
- **Write Emmy as already assented** — rejected; naming convergence is not bearer assent.
- **Manual one-file graph edit** — rejected; the four committed roster surfaces move atomically.
